item.title: Pachi Chintakaya Pachadi (Green Raw Tamarind Pickle) – Authentic Andhra-Style Recipe
item.description: Pachi Chintakaya Pachadi, also known as Green Raw Tamarind Pickle, is a treasured traditional dish from Andhra Pradesh. For generations, Telugu households have prepared this tangy, spicy, aromatic pachadi, often enjoying it with hot steamed rice topped with ghee. India is a country of countless pickles, but this dish stands out because of its sh
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: Saffron: The Most Expensive Spice in the World – History, Price, Cultivation & Why It’s So Valuable
item.description: Saffron is widely known as the most expensive spice in the world when measured by weight. Recognized for its deep golden color, calming aroma, and subtle yet distinctive flavor, saffron has been treasured across cuisines and cultures for over 3,000 years. Even today, despite modern agriculture and global trade, saffron continues to command exceptionally high prices — ofte
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: How to Cook Using Solar Power at Home: DIY Solar Cooking Guide
item.description: On PeoplesBLOG, we were writing articles related to Eco friendly homes and how to use Solar energy and along the same lines, in this article we are going to see how people can cook their food with Solar energy. Generally majority of the people nowadays use Gas stoves for Cooking at home and Solar is one of the best case of the replacement for this. People can make a major change on the
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: Andhra‑Style Vegetable Biryani Recipe: Easy & Flavorful
item.description: Here's a recipe for Vegetable Biryani in Andhra style: Ingredients: For the rice: 2 cups basmati rice 4 cups water 1 bay leaf 2 green
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: Gongura Pappu Recipe: Authentic Andhra Style Lentil Dish
item.description: Here's a recipe for Gongura Pappu, a popular Andhra-style lentil curry made with Gongura leaves (sorrel leaves): Ingredients: 1 cup toor dal (split pigeon peas) 2 cups Gongura leaves, washed and chopped 1 onion, finely chopped 2 tomatoes, finely chopped 2 green chilies, slit lengthwise 1 teaspoon ginger-garl
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How Much Rice Does the Average Indian Eat Each Year?
item.description: Average yearly consumption of rice for a typical Indian varies based on factors such as region, cultural practices, dietary preferences and socioeconomic status. However, rice is a staple food in India a
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: What Percentage of Indians Are Vegetarian? Statistics & Insights
item.description: Approximately 30-40% of the population in India is estimated to follow a vegetarian diet. However, this percentage can vary depending on the region, cultural practices and religious beliefs
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: Growing Enough Food to Feed a Family Year-Round by Yourself
item.description: Growing enough food to feed a family for the whole year is a big dream—but it is absolutely possible with planning, the right crops, and smart use of your land. To make this work, you need to understand how much food your family needs, what you should plant, how to get maximum harvest from your plot, and how to preserve your extra produce for future months. This guide explains
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: How to prepare Mango Lassi: A Creamy Yogurt Drink with the Goodness of Ripe Mangoes
item.description: Mango Lassi is one of India’s most loved summer drinks—rich, creamy, and bursting with the tropical sweetness of ripe mangoes. Blended with smooth yogurt and lightly sweetened with sugar or honey, this drink is both refreshing and indulgent. Served chilled, it makes for a perfect companion to a spicy Indian meal or as a cooling treat on a hot day. As an experienced cook, I can assur
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare Dahi Vada: Soft Lentil Dumplings in Creamy Yogurt with Tangy Chutneys
item.description: Dahi Vada is one of those timeless Indian chaats that brings together a perfect balance of flavors—soft and spongy lentil dumplings (vadas) soaked in silky yogurt, topped with tangy
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare Kulfi: Traditional Indian Ice Cream with Saffron, Cardamom & Nuts
item.description: Kulfi is the quintessential Indian frozen dessert—rich, dense, and full of flavor. Unlike Western ice creams, kulfi is not whipped, which gives it a firm, creamy texture that slowly melts in your mouth. Flavored with aromatic saffron, fragrant cardamom, and crunchy nuts like pistachios and almonds, this age-old dessert is a favorite at Indian celebrations, street-side stalls, and summer feast
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare Tomato Soup
item.description: Tomato soup is a classic comfort food that is easy to prepare and perfect for any time of the year. Here’s a simple recipe to make delicious homemade tomato soup: Ingredients: 1 kg tomatoes, ripe and juicy, ro
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare Kala Khatta Gola
item.description: Kala Khatta Gola, also known as Kala Khatta Ice Gola or Black Currant Ice Gola, is a popular Indian street treat, especially during the summer. This refreshing dessert is made with crushed ice, flavored syrup and a blend of tangy and spicy seasonings. Here's how you can prepare Kala Khatta Gola at home: Ingredients: For the Kala Khatta Syrup: 1 c
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: Pachi Pulusu Recipe - Andhra Style
item.description: Andhra Style Pachi Pulusu Recipe is a thin rasam made with freshly extracted tamarind water and mixed with sliced onions and freshly cut coriander leaves. Comfort food at its best when served with some steamed rice for a simple meal. It is a different way of creating a rasam that has been followed by the people from Andhra Pradesh and Telangana. This rasam is not heated and rather
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare Chana Chaat
item.description: Chana Chaat is a delicious, tangy and healthy Indian street food made with chickpeas, fresh vegetables and flavorful spices. It's quick to prepare and perfect as a snack or light meal. Here's a detailed recipe to prepare Chana Chaat at home: Ingr
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare Mango Pickle?
item.description: Making mango pickle, or aam ka achar, is a fun way to keep the tangy taste of raw mangoes using tasty spices. Here’s a simple recipe to make mango pickle at home: Ingredients: 3-4 raw mangoes (about 500 grams), firm and sour 1/2 cup mustard oil (or any cooking oil) 2 tablespoons mustard seeds 1 tablespoon fenugreek seeds 1 ta
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare Pudina Rice
item.description: Pudina Rice, also known as Mint Rice, is a flavorful and aromatic dish made with fresh mint leaves, rice and spices. It's a refreshing and light meal that can be served as a main dish or a side. Here's a detailed recipe to prepare Pudina Rice at home: Ingredients: For the Rice: 1 cup basmati rice 2 cups water 1 tablesp
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare Palak Patta Chaat
item.description: Palak Patta Chaat is a delightful Indian snack made with crispy spinach leaves topped with tangy and spicy chutneys, yogurt and sev. It's a wonderful combination of textures and flavors, making it a popular stre
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare gobi manchurian at home - Gobi Manchurian Recipe
item.description: Here's a recipe for Gobi Manchurian, a popular Indo-Chinese dish made with cauliflower: Ingredients: For the batter: 1 medium-sized cauliflower, cut into florets 1/2 c
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare Green Mango Salad
item.description: Green Mango Salad, also known as Som Tum in Thai cuisine, is a refreshing and tangy dish that combines the tartness of green mangoes with the crunch of vegetables and the kick of spices. It's perfect as a light meal or a side dish. Here’s how to prepare
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: Ragi roti with tomato chutney - Andhra Style
item.description: Here's a recipe for Ragi Roti with Tomato Chutney in Andhra style: #1 For Ragi Roti: 1 cup ragi flour (finger millet flour) 1/4 cup finely chopped onions 1/4 cup grated carrots 2 green chilies, finely chopped 2 tablespoons chopped coriander leaves Salt to taste Water as needed Oil for cooking
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare Pani Puri
item.description: Pani Puri is a fun and delicious twist on the classic Indian street food, Pani Puri (or Golgappa). This snack involves small, crispy puris filled with a tangy, spicy and flavorful water (pani) along with a savory filling. Here's how to prepare Pani Puri: Ingredients: For the Puris: 1 packet of ready-made puris (available at Indian grocery stores) or y
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: Dal Bhat Recipe - Nepali Style
item.description: Dal Bhat or Nepali Dal Bhat, is a dish made of lentils and rice, actually It is considered that this is the national dish of Nepal. Generally the rice is either boiled or steamed and It is served in the middle of the dish and surrounded by the dal or lentils and sometimes with some other curries or chutney or with a pickle. For people, Dal Bhat is easy to cook and it can be cooked with
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare Pasta at home
item.description: Here's a simple recipe for making pasta at home: Ingredients: 8 ounces (225 grams) dried pasta (such as spaghetti, penne, or fettuccine) Water for boiling Salt for seasoning Olive oil (optional) Instructions: Fill a large pot with water and bring it to a boil over high heat. Add a generous amount of salt to the bo
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to cook Onion Rava Dosa at home - Onion Rava Dosa Recipe
item.description: Onion Rava Dosa is a popular South Indian crepe made with semolina (rava), rice flour, and a mix of spices and onions. It is thin, crispy, and doesn't require fermentation, making it a quick and tasty meal option. Ingredients 1 cup semolina (rava) 1/2 cup rice flour 1/4 cup all-purpose flour (maida) 1 medium onion, finely chopp
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare Paneer Tikka?
item.description: Paneer Tikka is a well-loved Indian starter made with paneer (a type of Indian cheese) that is marinated in spices and yogurt, then grilled or baked for a smoky, tasty treat. Here's how you can make it at home in easy-to-understand language: Ingredients: 250 grams paneer, cut into cubes ½ cup thick yogurt (hung curd) 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare Kesar Pista Milkshake
item.description: Kesar Pista Milkshake is a rich and creamy Indian beverage made with saffron (kesar), pistachios (pista), and chilled milk. This royal drink is not only a treat for the taste buds but also packed with nutritional benefits. It’s perfect for summers, festive occasions, or just when you're in the mood for something indulgent and refreshing. Ingredients 2 cups chilled full
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: Growing enough Onions to Feed a Family by Yourself
item.description: Onions are easy to grow, which makes it one of the best plants for a beginning gardener. They need less attention during the growth when compared to other plants. Onions will be hav
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: How to prepare Moong Dal Salad
item.description: Moong Dal Salad is a light, protein-packed, and flavorful dish that makes for a perfect snack or side option. It is easy to prepare and requires minimal ingredients, making it an excellent choice for anyone seeking a healthy and delicious meal. Here’s how you can make this refreshing salad at home. Ingredients To prepare this wholesome salad, you will need:
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare Kokum Sherbet
item.description: Kokum Sherbet is a tangy and refreshing drink made from kokum (Garcinia indica), a fruit commonly used in Indian cuisine, especially in coastal regions. It's not only delicious but also has cooling properties, making it a perfect drink for hot weather. Here’s a simple recipe to prepare Kokum Sherbet: Ingredients: 1/2 cup dried kokum (about 8-10 pieces)
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare Curd Rice?
item.description: Curd Rice is a popular South Indian dish known for its simplicity and soothing flavors. It's often served as a comfort food and is particularly cooling during hot weather. Here's a simple recipe to prepare Curd Rice: Ingredients: 1 cup cooked rice (preferably short-grain rice like Sona Masuri or any leftover rice) 1 cup plain yogurt (curd), whisked until
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare Sattu Drink?
item.description: Sattu Drink is a traditional and nutritious beverage from the Indian subcontinent, particularly popular in the states of Bihar and Uttar Pradesh. It's made with roasted gram flour (sattu), water and spices and it's known for its cooling and energizing properties, especially during hot summers. Here's a simple recipe to prepare Sattu Drink: Ingredients: 3 tablespo
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare Kokum Rasam
item.description: Kokum Rasam is a tangy and flavorful South Indian soup made with kokum extract, tomatoes, spices and other aromatic ingredients. Here's a simple recipe to prepare Kokum Rasam: Ingredients: 1 tablespoon kokum extract (kokum juice) 2 medium-sized tomatoes, chopped 1 teaspoon cumin seeds 1 teaspoon black peppercorns 1/2 teaspoon m
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: How to prepare Mango Pickle at Home - Mango Pickle Recipe
item.description: Mango pickle is a popular Indian condiment made with raw mangoes and spices. Mango pickle is a variety of pickles prepared using mango. It is very popular in South and Southeast Asia. The pickling process in India differs from other re
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare Kairi Ki Sabzi?
item.description: Here's a simple recipe to prepare Kairi Ki Sabzi, a delicious curry made with raw mangoes: Ingredients: 2 medium-sized raw mangoes (kairi), peeled and chopped into cubes 2 tablespoons oil 1 teaspoon mustard seeds 1/2 teaspoon fenugreek seeds (methi seeds) 1/2 teaspoon asafoetida (hing) 1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder 1 t
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare a Cucumber Mint Cooler?
item.description: Cucumber Mint Cooler is a refreshing drink perfect for hot summer days. Here’s a simple recipe to prepare it at home: Ingredients: 1 large cucumber, peeled and chopped 10-12 fresh mint leaves Juice of 1 lemon 2-3
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare Stuffed Bell Peppers?
item.description: Stuffed bell peppers filled with spicy potato filling are a delicious and satisfying dish. Here’s a step-by-step recipe to prepare stuffed bell peppers: Ingredients: 4 large bell peppers (any color you prefer) 3-4 medium-sized potatoes, boiled and mashed 1 onion, finely chopped 2 green chilies, finely chopped (adjust to taste)
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare Moong Dal Khichdi
item.description: Moong Dal Khichdi is a healthy and comforting dish made with rice, split yellow moong dal (lentils) and mild spices. It’s easy to digest and is especially enjoyed during cold weather or when you're not feeling well. Here’s a simple recipe for making Moong Dal Khichdi: Ingredients: 1 cup rice (basmati rice works well) 1/2 cup split yellow moong dal (l
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: Researchers solve riddle of plant immune system
item.description: How do plants build resilience? An international research team led by the University of Gottingen studied the molecular mechanisms of the plant immune system. They were able to show a connection between a relatively unknown gene and resistance to pathogens. The results of the study were published in the journal The Plant Cell. Scientists from "PRoTECT
item.dc:creator: Jyothi Karthik D

item.title: Raw Mango Chutney - Andhra Style
item.description: Raw mango chutney is a flavorful condiment made with raw mangos, coriander leaves, onions and some spices. Raw Mango Chutney is a must-try recipe when raw mangoes are in season. Raw mangoes are packed with minerals and vitamins and are good to beat the summer heat. This raw mango chutney aids digestion and enhances the taste, aroma and nutrition of any dish. We usually have this raw man
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: Oranges - Let us know how to peel off & eat; along with its benefits
item.description: Ever heard of that name, it’s a fruit which helps us to boost our immunity levels and lowers the risk of diseases. In this lockdown time, one day I felt very tired, couldn’t continue my routine life and coincidently, all my family members were affected with some or other illness like headache, cough, cold, backache and tiredness to do mundane things. When we all went to the
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: How to prepare JalJeera
item.description: JalJeera is a traditional Indian summer drink known for its refreshing and tangy taste. Made with a blend of spices, herbs and tangy lemon, it is both a thirst quencher and an appetizer. Here's how you can prepare JalJeera at home: Ingredients: For the JalJeera Powder: 2 tablespoons cumin seeds 1 teaspoon black peppercorns
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare Dal Palak
item.description: Dal Palak is a nutritious and delicious dish made with lentils and spinach. It's a popular Indian recipe that combines the health benefits of dal (lentils) and palak (spinach) into a flavorful and wholesome meal.
item.dc:creator: Reddamma D

item.title: How to prepare Dhokla
item.description: Dhokla is a popular Gujarati snack that is steamed and made from fermented rice and chickpea flour (besan). It's light, spongy and usually served with green chutney. Here's a detailed recipe to prepare
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: How to prepare Sattu Paratha
item.description: Sattu Paratha is a delicious and nutritious stuffed flatbread popular in the Indian states of Bihar, Jharkhand and Uttar Pradesh. It is made with sattu (roasted gram flour) and various spices, creating a savory filling wrapped in a whole whea
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: How to prepare Tamarind Rice
item.description: The tamarind rice dish you're referring to sounds like Tamarind Rice, also known as Puliyodarai or Pulihora, which is a popular South Indian dish. Here's a recipe to prepare it: Ingredients: For the Rice: 1 cup rice (preferably sona masoori or any medium-grain rice) 2 cups water A pinch of salt For the Tamarind Paste:
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: How to prepare Mango Coconut Rice
item.description: Mango Coconut Rice is a delicious and fragrant dish made with ripe mangoes, coconut milk and aromatic spices. Here's how to prepare it: Ingredients: 1 cup basmati rice, washed and soaked for 30 minutes 1 ripe mango, peeled, pitted and diced 1 cup coconut milk 1/2 cup water 1 tablespoon oil or ghee 1 teaspoon mustard se
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: How to prepare Methi Thepla
item.description: Methi Thepla is a popular Gujarati flatbread made with fresh fenugreek leaves, whole wheat flour and spices. It's a delicious and nutritious dish that can be enjoyed as a snack or as part of a meal. Here's how to prepare Methi Thepla: Ingredients: 1 cup whole wheat flour (atta) 1 cup fresh fenugreek leaves (methi), washed and finely chopped 2 ta
item.dc:creator: Karthik Kumar D K

item.title: How to prepare Coconut Ladoo?
item.description: Coconut ladoo is a delicious Indian sweet made from grated coconut, condensed milk and cardamom. Here's a simple recipe to prepare coconut ladoo: Ingredients: 2 cu

item.title: Different spices are used to make a garam masala mix?
item.description: Garam masala is a quintessential spice blend used in Indian cuisine. It adds warmth, depth and complexity to dishes and its composition can vary widely based on regional preferences and personal taste. Here's a breakdown of the different
